body:
Network streaming server that broadcasts live telemetry from the driver's PC to connected engineer clients. Uses a TCP control channel for handshake/channel negotiation and UDP for low-latency telemetry frames.

### Protocol (`lmupi/streaming/protocol.py`)
- UDP telemetry frame: version, sequence number, timestamp, LZ4/zlib-compressed channel data (kept < 1400 bytes to avoid fragmentation)
- TCP control messages: handshake, channel subscription, heartbeat, goodbye — JSON payloads with a fixed 6-byte header
- Fallback from LZ4 to zlib if `lz4` is not installed

### Server (`lmupi/streaming/server.py`)
- `TelemetryStreamingServer(QThread)` — runs alongside `TelemetryReader`
- TCP listener with non-blocking `select` loop for client accept + control messages
- Per-client channel subscription filtering on UDP broadcast
- Heartbeat timeout (15s) for stale client eviction
- Configurable `port` (default `19740`) and `bind_address` (default `0.0.0.0`)

### Dashboard integration (`dashboard.py`)
- Port input, Start/Stop Stream button, status label (client count + data rate)
- Telemetry broadcast hooked into the existing refresh cycle — snapshots all channel values each tick

```python
from lmupi.streaming.server import TelemetryStreamingServer

server = TelemetryStreamingServer(port=19740)
server.start_server()
server.broadcast_telemetry({"Speed": 280.5, "RPM": 7200.0})
server.stop_server()
```

### Tests
- 18 protocol round-trip tests (UDP frames, TCP control messages, edge cases)
- 5 server integration tests (lifecycle, handshake, UDP delivery)

> 
> ## Requirements
> - Runs alongside TelemetryReader as a separate thread
> - UDP broadcast or unicast to connected clients
> - TCP listener for client handshake and channel negotiation
> - Configurable port (default: 19740)
> - Start/stop from dashboard UI
> - Server status indicator (clients connected, data rate out)
> - Bind to specific network interface or all interfaces
>
